items.title: OPEC+ set for fourth oil quota hike since Hormuz closure, sources say
items.summary: Seven core members of OPEC+, ‌which groups OPEC and allied producers including Russia, have ​increased their output quotas from April to June by almost 600,000 barrels per day

items.title: Nepal not asking for mediation from third parties on border dispute with India: Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal
items.summary: ‘A completely new political reality in Nepal,’ says Shisir Khanal, indicating more high-level visits on the cards but gives no timeline for Prime Minister Balen Shah’s visit plans for India

items.title: Russian drone hit nuclear-fuel storage facility near Chornobyl, says Ukraine
items.summary: In separate statements, ‌Kyiv’s General Staff and the state atomic agency said ‌a container-receiving building had ‌been partially ⁠destroyed, but that no spent ⁠fuel had been stored there at the time of the attack

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Israel Army says intercepted two projectiles fired from Lebanon
items.summary: U.S. says it destroyed two Iran drones targeting Hormuz shippin; Pakistan’s Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi in Tehran to push for peace amidst rising tensions

items.title: Missing Malaysian hiker's rescue hailed a 'miracle'
items.summary: When we heard that she had been found safe, it felt like our prayers had been answered; it is truly a miracle, said the rescued hiker’s friend

items.title: One person killed, five injured in shooting in central Israel, ambulance service says
items.summary: The ‌shootings took place in three separate areas in central Israel close to the Palestinian West Bank city of Qalqilya.

items.title: Hyderabad man shot dead during pizza delivery in Philadelphia
items.summary: According to information shared with them, he had been assigned a delivery to a deserted location which later appeared to have been a setup

items.title: Armenians go to the polls under Russian pressure aimed at preventing a drift toward West
items.summary: Armenians are voting in parliamentary elections as the government seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and increase cooperation with the West

items.title: India's energy demand boom to reshape global markets, says Rosneft chief
items.summary: Today, India's economy is one of the key drivers of global energy consumption growth, says Igor Sechin, chief executive of Russian oil producer Rosneft

items.title: U.S. says shot down Iran drones in fresh escalation
items.summary: U.S. CENTCOM said it destroyed two Iranian drones "that threatened international maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz", hours after announcing it struck four other drones and coastal surveillance radar sites

items.title: U.S. VP Vance blames British student's murder on migrant 'invasion'
items.summary: "Henry Nowak died the same way a civilisation dies: abandoned, handcuffed by authorities who neither trusted nor cared for him, and accused of hate crimes he did not commit," says VP Vance

items.summary: While we are working with ESO and National Grid for the new electrical infrastructure, National Grid has formally alerted that the connectivity project is delayed, says Koushik Chatterjee, ED and CFO, Tata Steel.

items.title: Beaufort Castle | Besieged history
items.summary: A 12th-century hilltop castle has emerged as a key strategic flashpoint in Israel’s war with Hezbollah in occupied southern Lebanon

items.summary: During his visit, Mr. Naqvi will hold talks with various top officials, including Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, Iran's semi-official news agency Tasnim reported.

items.title: Nearly 500 confirmed cases in Central Africa Ebola outbreak: WHO
items.summary: In its daily update on the situation, the World Health Organisation tallied 452 confirmed cases, including 82 deaths, in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where the outbreak was declared three weeks ago

items.title: Trump urges new spy chief to fire employees

items.title: Taiwan says Chinese coast guard, research ships near key S. China Sea islands
items.summary: The islands between southern Taiwan ⁠and Hong Kong are seen by some security experts as vulnerable to Chinese attack due to their distance — more than ​400 km (250 miles) — from ‌Taiwan island

items.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian baby in occupied West Bank, say health officials
items.summary: Israeli soldiers accused of harming Palestinians are rarely penalised and were indicted in fewer than 1% of cases based on 2,427 complaints alleging wrongdoing between 2016 and 2024, according to Israeli rights group Yesh Din

items.title: Bill in U.S. Congress seeks to end H-1B visa as route to green card
items.summary: The legislation comes amid the Trump administration's crackdown on legal migration programmes through tightening restrictions, prioritisation of higher wages for H-1B applicants, and imposition of a $1,00,000 fee on new petitions.

items.title: Watch: Cabinet Trouble for D.K. Shivakumar as Ramalinga Reddy Resigns | Above the Fold | 05.06.2026
items.summary: In today’s episode, we look at Karnataka Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar’s first political crisis, as Ramalinga Reddy resigns from Cabinet. We also ask our reporters to break down what K. Annamalai’s latest announcement means for Tamil Nadu politics.

items.title: UN doubles appeal for Lebanon aid to nearly $640 mn amid Israel war
items.summary: Three months into the war, the United Nations warned that a "humanitarian catastrophe" was looming in Lebanon, with a quarter of its population needing aid

items.title: Palestinians call on Trump to stop Israeli annexation
